sample.description:

sample.title: How Data Centers in Space Could Change AI
sample.description:
“Data centers in space” may sound like science fiction, but as the SpaceX IPO augurs a space boom, tech giants and startups are betting orbital artificial intelligence is the next frontier.

How to Build a Data Center in Space: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn
Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos plan to make AI extraterrestrial.

Building data centers in space is still a relatively nascent idea, but it’s gaining traction and financial backing, as energy and space constraints limit the expansion of massive computing facilities here on Earth. Earlier this year, in the latest leg of their decades-long space race, Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin both announced plans to build and launch so-called orbital data centers.

Learn more: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn

00:00-01:36 The AI data center squeeze
01:37-03:43 Starcloud’s big ambition
03:44-04:17 Why data centers in space make sense

sample.title: Dario Amodei Addresses Reports of Claude’s Role in US Military Strikes
sample.description:
On The Circuit with Emily Chang, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei addresses reports that the US military is using Claude as part of the US-Israel war with Iran, including during a strike in Iran that destroyed a girls' school, killing more than 150 people. 

Amodei concedes "terrible" mistakes will be made but defends the integration of AI in defense by emphasizing that "a human makes the final call."

sample.title: Inside Indonesia's Market Meltdown
sample.description:
Indonesia was once seen as a darling of investors. At $1.5 trillion, Indonesia’s GDP is more than that of Singapore and Thailand combined. Until recently, Indonesia’s stock exchange was the biggest in the region. 

But fears of corruption and policy missteps in markets and government under President Prabowo Subianto are raising concern about the country’s economic future.

sample.title: Why Abercrombie Is Copying Aritzia's Playboook
sample.description:
Abercrombie & Fitch's torrid growth has started to slow. To counteract moderating sales and increasing competition, the retailer is selling third-party shoe brands like Puma, Frye, Hunter and GH Bass. CNBC's Gabrielle Fonrouge reports.

sample.title: Where Markets Think Fed Chair Warsh Is Taking Interest Rates
sample.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is floating major revisions to the central bank’s management of assets, inflation and public communications. Markets are pricing in higher odds for future rate hikes, rather than rate cuts as emphasized by Chair Warsh in confirmation hearings. CNBC’s Matt Peterson, Steve Liesman and Jeff Cox report.

sample.title: Can AI Compute Become The Next Big Futures Market?
sample.description:
For decades, companies have turned to futures markets to manage uncertainty. Airlines hedge fuel costs. Farmers hedge crops. Manufacturers hedge metals. Now a startup wants to bring that same financial machinery to artificial intelligence. Silicon Data and CME Group are working on futures contracts tied to GPU rental prices, a market that could help firms hedge rising AI infrastructure, cloud computing and data center costs. AI companies spend heavily to rent high-end GPU power for training and running artificial intelligence models, but future compute prices can be hard to predict.  CNBC's Yun Li reports.

05:28-07:21 Chapter 3: Setting a Benchmark

sample.title: Why KFC Has Fallen Behind In The U.S.
sample.description:
Chains like Chick-fil-A and Raising Cane's have become fast food mainstays as Americans continue to crave chicken. Yet KFC has become the fourth-largest U.S. chicken chain by market share despite the company saying that ‘it made chicken famous.’ The U.S. has also slipped to its third-largest market behind China and Europe. Now it's rebranding its logos, menu and restaurants as it hopes to reinvigorate sales and cut through the crowded fast-food sector.  CNBC’s Amelia Lucas has more on the company’s innovations.

Produced Shot and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Reporting by: Amelia Lucas
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Getty Images, KFC

sample.title: What To Expect From Trump’s Trip To The G7 Summit In France
sample.description:
President Trump is set to travel to France for the upcoming G7 summit, which will take place from June 15-17. Markets will be watching for signs of stability as the summit takes place against the backdrop of the Iran war, the Russia-Ukraine war and tensions between the U.S. and Europe over tariffs. CNBC’s Megan Cassella breaks down what to expect from the summit.

Reporter: Megan Cassella
Produced by: Macklin Fishman, Ashlee Trujillo
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin

sample.title: Kevin Warsh says Fed had a 'family fight' when discussing policy decision
sample.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh reiterated his hope for a "family fight" as the Fed decides on policy during Wednesday's press conference.

The Fed chairman has previously used the phrase to describe how he believes how discussions and decisions within the central bank should be done.

sample.title: Kevin Warsh says the Fed has struggled to communicate its commitment to lower inflation
sample.description:
Chairman Warsh reiterated that the Fed is committed to bringing inflation back down to 2%.

“The commitment to deliver is strong, unanimous, and unambiguous, and that’s I think an important message we’ve missed for five years, and we’re going to fix that.”

sample.title: Trump's support in rural America slips
sample.description:
According to a recent Reuters/Ipsos poll, rural disapproval of President Trump rose, driven by concerns over the cost of living and the economy. Poll shows only 31% of rural respondents approve Trump's handling of economic issues. Leah Douglas breaks down the numbers and what’s at play.
 
#trump #donaldtrump #voters #politics #ruralamerica

sample.title: Meet Merlin the duck: Mexico's unofficial World Cup mascot
sample.description:
A domesticated duck named Merlin became an unlikely star on the streets of Mexico City as World Cup fever grips the capital.
 
Karla Ivette Gomez, who describes herself as "Merlin's mum," told Reuters the attention came as a complete surprise, adding that she and her family are "very pleased" that people embraced the duck as the country's unofficial mascot.

sample.title: Trump says Iran deal will be signed 'shortly' | BBC News
sample.description:
US President Donald Trump has said the framework for the Iran deal, which was set to be signed on Friday, could be signed as early as Thursday. 
 
Trump also said the US could resume attacks if the agreement doesn't work out - "If they don't behave, we'll go right back to dropping bombs right smack in the middle of their head."
 
Three Iranian oil tankers have passed through a US blockade for the first time in two months. Around 600 vessels are currently stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. 
 
The memorandum agreement states the Strait will be toll free for 60 days. 
 
Israeli forces have carried out new strikes in southern Lebanon, state media say, despite renewed criticism from US President Donald Trump of Israel's actions in the country.
 
Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#US #Iran #Israel #BBCNews

sample.title: SpaceX overtakes Amazon as world's fifth most valuable company. #BBCNews
Russian warship fires warning shots near boat off UK coast | Global News Podcast
sample.description:
A retired British couple who were on a yacht which had warning shots fired near it by a Russian warship in the English Channel said the experience was "surreal". 
 
Russia’s defence ministry said the yacht was on a “dangerous course” and that several attempts were made to contact it. 
 
This came hours after the UK government imposed further sanctions on Russian businesses and on ships accused of illegally transporting Russian oil and gas. 
 
It also followed the announcement that the UK defence minister had resigned over the UK government's lack of spending on defence and security.
 
We speak to the BBC's diplomatic correspondent, Paul Adams, about the incident and the alleged security risks posed to the UK by Russia. 
 
New episodes of the Global News Podcast are published twice a day. 
 
You can listen here: https://link.mgln.ai/GNP-YT
 
And you can find more of our YouTube episodes here: https://bbc.in/GlobalNewsPodcast 
 
#UK #Russia #BBCNews

sample.title: Trump says deal with Iran will be signed "shortly". #DonaldTrump #Iran #BBCNews
Trump tells leaders 'I'm the boss' - after claim he's changed position on Ukraine | BBC News
sample.description:
As the G7 summit comes to a close in France, Donald Trump said he had a very good meeting with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky and that Russia should "make a deal".

The US president has also told journalists that the US is considering whether to reimpose sanctions on Russia.

The US has suspended sanctions on Russian oil in an effort to bring down global energy prices, which soared as a result of its war with Iran. Oil prices fell recently after the two sides agreed a deal.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news
